topic: humanoid foundation models: world models, VLAs, robot brains, embodied AI research, model releases, training data, simulation, compute

items.title: Friction is the input robot world models are missing
items.summary: Vision and joint encoders can't capture contact physics, argues Contactile CEO Heba Khamis — she proposes a 'vision-μ-action' class of models conditioned on real-time friction measurement, shifting world models from statistical pattern-matching toward causal understanding of why grasps succeed or fail.

items.title: Generalist AI puts GEN-1 to work on Elite's cobots
items.summary: The Nvidia-backed startup founded by ex-DeepMind researchers will use Elite Robots' industrial cobots for real-world data collection and validation of its GEN-1 foundation model — a pairing that has already logged 1,800+ block stackings with zero interventions at a 99% task success rate, according to Elite Robots.

items.title: Tencent stacks three robot brains into its Tairos platform
items.summary: At WAIC in Shanghai, Tencent rolled out a full-stack embodied intelligence offering — pairing the Hy-Embodied-VLA-0.5 action model with the VLM-1.0 perception and RxBrain-1.0 reasoning models under a new TairosAgent framework, according to Tencent. The Tairos platform, first launched in 2025, now ships open-source tools and cloud services for robot developers.

items.title: Flexion's CEO warns robotics is stuck in the teleoperation trap
items.summary: Teleop datasets are over 100,000 times smaller than what trains today's language models, Nikita Rudin argues — and reinforcement learning, not an endless stream of human demonstrations, is the way out.

items.title: Sunday calls laundry 'solved' as ACT-2 folds at 99%
items.summary: The Memo maker's new foundation model folded clothes in homes it had never seen at a 99.1% success rate — 778 of 785 attempts, zero-shot with no per-home fine-tuning, according to Sunday Robotics, though the figures are the company's own evaluation. ACT-2 was pre-trained on manipulation data from $200 skill-capture gloves shipped to more than 2,000 contributors.

items.title: Agility opens a 60,000 sq ft Silicon Valley hub to train Digit
items.summary: The Digit maker's new Fremont center will focus on training, testing and advancing the AI that teaches its humanoid new skills, according to Agility — with nearly 200 AI and field-operations hires planned and more than $300 million in multi-year Digit orders behind the expansion.

items.title: LimX unveils COSA 0.5 — and Oli runs household chores with no teleop
items.summary: The freshly funded Shenzhen maker's three-layer 'agentic OS' pairs a world model and closed-loop reasoning with VLA skills — enough, according to LimX, for China's first fully autonomous long-horizon home run by a full-size humanoid.

items.title: Jim Fan: 'Robotics right now is like LLMs in 2020'
items.summary: NVIDIA's AI director told RAISE Week in Paris that humanoid prices have fallen 98% in two decades and open GR00T downloads have passed 10 million, according to Fan — with 'world action models' that predict future frames as the next paradigm.

items.title: 1X launches a World Model Lab to scale humanoid intelligence
items.summary: The NEO maker's new research organization, headed by ex-Luma researcher Samarth Sinha, will pretrain on web media, human video, simulation and robot data — world models, says CEO Bernt Børnich, will free humanoids from narrow tasks in static environments.
